name: NumberSpinner.DecimalNumber

aliases.name: DecimalNumber
aliases.comment: Type alias for the Decimal library (to make the choice of library opaque at this point)

values.name: add
values.comment: Add two decimal numbers

values.name: decimalDigits
values.comment: Return just the decimal digits of the decimal number (as chars, since we don't do any calculations with it, we jut display it)

values.name: fromFloat
values.comment: Convert from a floating point value. This function is a bit weird, since it uses Decimal.fromFloat, which can fail. I don't know when this fails. Hopefully, returning 0 in that case is fine.

values.name: fromInt
values.comment: Convert from integer (meaning have 0 as decimal part)

values.name: fromString
values.comment: Convert from a string

values.name: gt
values.comment: Implement "greater than"

values.name: integralPart
values.comment: For a decimal number, return the integral part (the part before the decimal point). NOTE: This is not infinite precision now, of course. If we have more than 2^32 (or something) as integer, this will fail (and return -1337, somewhat randomly)

values.name: lt
values.comment: Implement "less than"

values.name: mul
values.comment: Multiply two decimal numbers

values.name: subtract
values.comment: Subtract two decimal numbers

values.name: tenToThePower
values.comment: Implement 10^i which is used to change integral digits of a number

values.name: tenToThePowerMinus
values.comment: Implement 10^(-i) which is used to change decimal digits of a number

values.name: toFloat
values.comment: Return a floating-point representation of the number. Use with caution, of course. But might be fine to send the number over the wire (where precision can be safely lost - Tango uses floats anyways, for example)

values.name: toString
values.comment: Convert decimal to string

name: NumberSpinner.DigitalNumber

unions.name: DigitalNumber
unions.comment: Represents a decimal number which has bounds on the decimal places and the value contained

aliases.name: DecimalType
aliases.comment: Type alias for the decimal type used

values.name: decimalChars
values.comment: Return the decimal characters for the number (as chars, so just for display)

values.name: decreaseDecimalDigit
values.comment: Decrease the d'th decimal digit of the number (taking care of under/overflow)

values.name: decreaseIntegerDigit
values.comment: Decrease the d'th integer digit of the number (taking care of under/overflow)

values.name: decreaseSign
values.comment: Flip the sign (yeah, increase/decrease is weirdly named)

values.name: getValue
values.comment: Retrieve just the value of the digital number

values.name: hasDecimals
values.comment: Check if the number has decimal places at all. If not, we can omit the ".xxx" and make the input field more narrow.

values.name: hasSign
values.comment: Check if the number has a sign (used to determine if we display a plus/minus in front of it and making that changeable)

values.name: increaseDecimalDigit
values.comment: Increase the d'th decimal digit of the number (taking care of under/overflow)

values.name: increaseIntegerDigit
values.comment: Increase the d'th integer digit of the number (taking care of under/overflow)

values.name: increaseSign
values.comment: Flip the sign (yeah, increase/decrease is weirdly named)

values.name: integerChars
values.comment: Return the integral characters for the number (as chars, so just for display)

values.name: isNegative
values.comment: Check if the number is currently negative

values.name: make
values.comment: Create a digital number (information hiding here, since we don't expose the type constructor directly)

values.name: modifyValue
values.comment: Helper function to modify the value of a digital number, taking care of not over/underflowing it

values.name: numberOfDecimalDigits
values.comment: Return the number of decimal digits of the number (which is fixed on construction of the digital number)

values.name: numberOfIntegerDigits
values.comment: Return the number of integral digits of the number (to display the correct number of digits in the UI)

values.name: replaceDecimalDigit
values.comment: Replace the whichDigit's integer digit with "newDigit" (so converting "123.45" to "123.95" or something)

values.name: replaceIntegerDigit
values.comment: Replace the whichDigit's integer digit with "newDigit" (so converting "123" to "193" or something)

values.name: valueAsFloat
values.comment: Convert the decimal number to the approximate floating point value

name: NumberSpinner.NumberSpinner

unions.name: CursorPosition
unions.comment: A type signifying where the cursor currently is, and where it can move.

aliases.name: Model
aliases.type: { number : NumberSpinner.DigitalNumber.DigitalNumber, cursorPosition : NumberSpinner.NumberSpinner.CursorPosition, hasFocus : Basics.Bool, toMsg : NumberSpinner.NumberSpinner.Msg -> msg }

aliases.name: SpinnerBounds
aliases.comment: We use this class for the "cursor left" and "cursor right" motions in conjunction with the CursorPosition type (see below). We want to define cursor movement without regards to the current number displayed. For movement, we need the left and right bounds, which we store here.
aliases.type: { boundsDecimals : Basics.Int, boundsIntegers : Basics.Int, hasSign : Basics.Bool }

values.name: getDecimalValue
values.comment: Get current decimal value of the spinner

values.name: init
values.comment:
Initialize the number spinner with the number of decimal places, the minimum bound, maximum bound, current value and a converter function into your message type. Example

    NumberSpinner.init
        -- two odecimal places
        2
        -- minimum value (inclusive)
        (Decimal.fromInt 0)
        -- maximum value (inclusive)
        (Decimal.fromInt 10)
        -- current value (inited from a string, which might fail because it's at runtime)
        (Maybe.withDefault (Decimal.fromInt 0) <| Decimal.fromString "1.23")
        -- convert to my custom SpinnerMessage
        SpinnerMessage
values.type: Basics.Int -> NumberSpinner.DecimalNumber.DecimalNumber -> NumberSpinner.DecimalNumber.DecimalNumber -> NumberSpinner.DecimalNumber.DecimalNumber -> (NumberSpinner.NumberSpinner.Msg -> msg) -> NumberSpinner.NumberSpinner.Model msg

values.name: isMutatingMsg
values.comment: Check if the incoming Msg actually mutates the decimal number value, or if it is just moving the cursor around, for example

values.name: moveCursorLeft
values.comment:
Modify cursor position to move to the left, if possible (skipping "boundaries" between sign, integral and decimal places)

This is exposed mainly for the tests.

values.name: moveCursorRight
values.comment:
Modify cursor position to move to the right, if possible (skipping "boundaries" between sign, integral and decimal places)

This is exposed mainly for the tests.

values.name: setValue
values.comment: Set current "digital number" value of the spinner

values.name: update
values.comment: Elm Architecture update function
values.type: NumberSpinner.NumberSpinner.Msg -> NumberSpinner.NumberSpinner.Model msg -> ( NumberSpinner.NumberSpinner.Model msg, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: valueAsFloat
values.comment: Convert the decimal number to the approximate floating point value

values.name: view
values.comment: Elm architecture view function
